hero.title: Certificate in Employee Relations Law
hero.description: Most HR professionals never receive formal employment law training. In 4½ days, you'll build comprehensive expertise in labor relations, discrimination defense, and workplace compliance. Practicing attorneys. Practical strategies. Confidence to handle complex situations.

contentSection.headline: Gain Confidence to Handle Complex Situations
contentSection.description: The Certificate in Employee Relations Law is 4.5 intensive days where you'll build practical employment law expertise with experienced attorneys - working through real workplace scenarios, not theoretical lectures.
benefits: Practicing attorneys, not academics – Your instructors handle employment law cases daily at firms like Taft, Thompson Hine, and Barnes & Thornburg. They teach from real courtrooms and boardrooms, not textbooks.

Comprehensive depth, not surface overviews – 4½ days of intensive coverage you cannot get from conference sessions, law firm CLEs, or online searches. This is the formal training most HR professionals lack.
Peer learning with HR professionals nationwide – Discussions with peers facing similar challenges provide insights you cannot find anywhere else.

A credential that advances your career – Join 80,000+ professionals who earned the IAML certificate. It demonstrates your expertise to employers and serves as evidence your organization takes employment law seriously.
Support that continues after you leave – 12 months of quarterly employment law updates, alumni resources, and faculty access keep you current as regulations change.

header.title: What You'll Master in 4½ Days
header.description: Build comprehensive expertise in labor relations, discrimination defense, and employment law compliance. Each block delivers practical strategies from practicing attorneys you can apply to your most pressing challenges the following week.

blocks.label: Block I
blocks.title: Comprehensive Labor Relations
blocks.description: Navigate the complex terrain of organizing risk, employee handbooks, union elections, and collective bargaining with proven strategies that protect your workforce while maintaining compliance.

competencyGroups.title: The Current NLRB & Organizing Landscape
competencyGroups.description: Key shifts in elections, handbooks, and joint-employer liability.

skills.title: Elections & recognition after NLRB Cemex decision
skills.description: Faster elections; higher risk of bargaining orders for ULPs; what 'card-check-adjacent' and new GC guidance mean in practice.

skills.title: Handbooks after NLRB Stericycle decision
skills.description: Why neutral rules (civility, confidentiality, social media, devices) can still violate §7; how to rewrite with context-specific justifications.

skills.title: Joint-employer status (where it stands now)
skills.description: 2023 rule invalidated; prior standard effectively back—implications for franchise/contracting models and vendor oversight.

competencyGroups.title: Practical Guidance for Non-Union Workplaces
competencyGroups.description: Reduce organizing heat with lawful hiring, onboarding, and training.

skills.title: Hiring & onboarding practices
skills.description: Lower ULP risk; orientation scripts that avoid 'captive audience' pitfalls.

skills.title: The three big sparks for organizing
skills.description: Scheduling/fairness, safety (including psychological safety), and transparency (wages/career paths)—how HR cools them with credible channels and swift fixes.

skills.title: Manager training for §7 in non-union settings
skills.description: Protected concerted activity on Slack/Teams and group chats; where discipline goes wrong under Stericycle.

competencyGroups.title: Election Readiness & Campaign Law
competencyGroups.description: Operate within accelerated timelines while preserving laboratory conditions.

skills.title: Campaign playbook: Do / Don't
skills.description: Practical rules for message discipline under accelerated timelines; maintaining true laboratory conditions.

skills.title: Access, equal time, and solicitation rules
skills.description: Apply solicitation/distribution and email/Teams policies—updated for hybrid work.

skills.title: Post-election objections and reruns
skills.description: Prevent and defend against objections to avoid costly reruns.

competencyGroups.title: Collective Bargaining
competencyGroups.description: Proposals that matter, documentation, and aligning handbooks with CBAs.

skills.title: Setting the table
skills.description: Management rights, grievance/arbitration, zipper, information requests, technology/data clauses, and safety.

skills.title: Good-faith bargaining
skills.description: Information requests, surface bargaining risks, and documenting proposals/counterproposals.

skills.title: Handbook ↔ CBA alignment
skills.description: Avoid conflicts and unintended past-practice creation.

competencyGroups.title: Strikes, Pickets, and Employer Response
competencyGroups.description: Protected vs unprotected actions, lawful responses, and contingency planning.

skills.title: Types of strikes and lockouts
skills.description: Intermittent/partial strike pitfalls; replacements and lawful lockout strategy.

skills.title: Picketing & social media amplification
skills.description: What changes in a viral era and how to respond lawfully.

skills.title: Contingency planning
skills.description: Checklists and communication protocols that minimize disruption.

blocks.label: Block II
blocks.title: Discrimination Prevention & Defense
blocks.description: Master discrimination law fundamentals across all protected classes. Learn how to identify workplace practices that expose your company to liability and implement preventive measures that strengthen your defense.

competencyGroups.title: Title VII: Discrimination, Harassment & Retaliation
competencyGroups.description: Trends, hybrid risks, LGBTQ+ protections, religion, and compliant DEI.

skills.title: Charge trends
skills.description: Higher filings; retaliation remains most prevalent—action items for intake, interim measures, and documentation.

skills.title: Harassment in hybrid workplaces
skills.description: Chats/DMs, meeting recordings, and third-party/customer harassment—practical controls.

skills.title: LGBTQ+ and sex-based claims post-Bostock; religious conflicts after Groff
skills.description: Document undue hardship for schedules/duties while ensuring equal treatment.

skills.title: Reverse-discrimination / DEI backlash
skills.description: Run compliant DEI—widen access, use merit-based selection, and lawful outreach—amid scrutiny.

competencyGroups.title: ADA, PWFA, FMLA: Accommodation & Leave in a Hybrid World
competencyGroups.description: Define essential functions, align performance, and coordinate overlapping laws.

skills.title: Defining essential functions with remote options
skills.description: Use defensible analyses and documented rationales.

skills.title: Mental-health accommodations and performance alignment
skills.description: Balance supports with expectations and safety.

skills.title: PWFA / PUMP intersections
skills.description: Scheduling and break implications; coordination with other laws.

skills.title: FMLA interference/retaliation traps
skills.description: Coordinate ADA/FMLA/Workers' Comp to avoid compounding liability.

competencyGroups.title: ADEA & Equal Pay
competencyGroups.description: RIF documentation and privileged, action-oriented pay audits.

skills.title: Disparate impact and RIF selection
skills.description: Build selection files that hold up and avoid age bias.

skills.title: Pay transparency and equal pay audits
skills.description: Structure privileged audits and communications.

competencyGroups.title: Resolving Claims Without Litigation
competencyGroups.description: Internal resolution, arbitration programs, and mediation tactics.

skills.title: Internal resolution
skills.description: Prompt, impartial investigations; what to share in outcome letters.

skills.title: Arbitration & class/collective waivers
skills.description: Enforceability trends; drafting updates for remote workers and electronic consent.

skills.title: Pre-charge settlements and mediation
skills.description: Best practices to resolve early with durable agreements.

blocks.label: Block III
blocks.title: Special Issues & Advanced Topics
blocks.description: Deep dive into complex employment law issues including wage and hour, wrongful discharge, workplace privacy, and post-employment restrictions.

competencyGroups.title: Wage & Hour (FLSA) Under Remote/Hybrid
competencyGroups.description: Off-the-clock time, hybrid travel, and complex pay calculations.

skills.title: Remote off-the-clock risk
skills.description: Email/IM, rounding, device sync; travel time for split/hybrid days; audits for 'player-coach' roles.

skills.title: Commission/bonus/draw plans
skills.description: Overtime calculations without tripping over spiffs and recoveries.

competencyGroups.title: Wrongful Discharge & Retaliation
competencyGroups.description: Documented timelines and protected-activity analysis that defeat pretext.

skills.title: 'At-will with evidence'
skills.description: Build the performance notes → PIP → termination timeline that defeats pretext.

skills.title: Protected activity lookback
skills.description: Adverse-action timing; manager speech and political/off-duty conduct.

competencyGroups.title: Workplace Privacy & Monitoring
competencyGroups.description: Employee data privacy and productivity monitoring in practice.

skills.title: Employee data privacy
skills.description: CPRA and state analogs: notices, access requests, retention/deletion, and disciplinary use.

skills.title: Monitoring productivity and keystrokes
skills.description: Geolocation, BYOD, and personal-account discovery—practical boundaries.

competencyGroups.title: Protecting Confidential Information & Mobility
competencyGroups.description: Post-employment restrictions after the FTC rule's vacatur.

skills.title: What remains enforceable
skills.description: NDAs, non-solicits, trade-secret law—how to draft so they stick.

skills.title: Garden leave and TRAPs
skills.description: Training-repayment agreements and choice-of-law strategy across states.

faq.question: Can I attend just one or two blocks instead of the full program?
faq.answer: Yes. While most participants get the best value from the complete 4½-day program, you can register for individual blocks based on your immediate needs. Block I (Labor Relations) and Block II (Discrimination Law) are each $1,375 individually, while Block III (Special Issues) is $575. The full program at $2,375 saves you $1,000 versus purchasing all blocks separately.

faq.question: What's the difference between in-person, virtual, and on-demand formats?
faq.answer: All three formats feature the same practicing attorney instructors, current content, and professional credits. In-person provides face-to-face networking and immediate interaction. Virtual delivers the same live instruction via Zoom with full Q&A capability. On-demand lets you learn at your own pace over 90 days with the ability to pause and revisit complex topics. Choose the format that fits your schedule and learning style—the quality and content remain identical.

faq.question: What happens after I register?
faq.answer: Within minutes, you'll receive an invoice email. Once payment is processed, you can schedule your complimentary pre-program consultation (10-15 minutes) to discuss your specific workplace challenges and identify which program content will have the biggest impact for you. Pre-program materials arrive one week before your session starts.

faq.question: What continuing education credits will I earn?
faq.answer: The complete program provides 29.75 SHRM/HRCI/CLE credits. Plus, your enrollment includes 12 months of quarterly employment law updates (4 sessions × 1.5 credits each = 6 additional credits), bringing your total to 35.75 credits—typically more than HR professionals earn in two years.

faq.question: How current is the content? When was it last updated?
faq.answer: Your materials are living resources that update continuously as employment law changes. Unlike static textbooks that become outdated immediately after publication, our content reflects current court decisions, regulatory changes, and compliance requirements. Recent updates include the NLRB's Cemex decision on organizing, the Stericycle handbook standards, post-Bostock LGBTQ+ protections, and state-specific AI hiring regulations.

faq.question: Why do you emphasize practicing attorneys, not academics?
faq.answer: Your instructors handle employment law cases, defend discrimination charges, and advise employers on these exact issues daily. They're not teaching from textbooks—they're sharing strategies that work in actual courtrooms and real workplaces. When you ask what do I do about a specific situation, they answer from direct experience, not theory.

faq.question: Does the $2,375 program fee include everything?
faq.answer: Yes. Your enrollment includes all live instruction, complete program materials, 29.75 continuing education credits, pre-program consultation, 12 months of quarterly employment law updates (4 sessions, $1,588 value), permanent access to living resources, and alumni benefits for all future programs. There are no hidden fees or additional charges.
